proven.claim: PROVEN: isVacuous returns false when no declare-const and no define-fun lines exist
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 59
; PROVEN: isVacuous returns false when no declare-const and no define-fun lines exist
(declare-const declares_count Int)
(declare-const define_funs_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Preconditions model the input that hits the first guard
(assert (= declares_count 0))
(assert (= define_funs_count 0))
; Code transition: guard `declares.length === 0 && defineFuns.length === 0` fires → return false
(assert (=> (and (= declares_count 0) (= define_funs_count 0)) (= result 0)))
; Negate the property: can result ever be true (1)?
(assert (not (= result 0)))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: isVacuous returns true when declarations exist but no assert lines are present
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 59
; PROVEN: isVacuous returns true when declarations exist but no assert lines are present
(declare-const declared_size Int)
(declare-const asserts_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Preconditions: passes the first guard (has declarations), hits the second guard
(assert (> declared_size 0))
(assert (= asserts_count 0))
; Code transition: `if (asserts.length === 0) return true`
(assert (=> (and (> declared_size 0) (= asserts_count 0)) (= result 1)))
; Negate the property: can result ever be false (0)?
(assert (not (= result 1)))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: isVacuous returns true when declaredNames.size < 2, even with assertions present
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 59
; PROVEN: isVacuous returns true when declaredNames.size < 2, even with assertions present
(declare-const declared_size Int)
(declare-const asserts_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Preconditions: has declarations and assertions (passes first two guards),
; but only 1 unique name survived the Set deduplication
(assert (>= declared_size 1))
(assert (>= asserts_count 1))
(assert (= declared_size 1))
; Code transition: `if (declaredNames.size < 2) return true`
(assert (=> (and (>= asserts_count 1) (< declared_size 2)) (= result 1)))
; Negate: can result be false (0) with a single declared name?
(assert (not (= result 1)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: declared variable name collides with SMT-LIB keyword;
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 59
; VIOLATION: declared variable name collides with SMT-LIB keyword;
; regex counts keyword-as-operator as a variable reference, inflating referencedVars
(declare-const declared_count Int)
(declare-const real_var_refs Int)
(declare-const keyword_op_refs Int)
(declare-const referenced_vars_counted Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Code transition: declaredNames = {"not", "x"}, size = 2 → passes size guard
(assert (= declared_count 2))
(assert (>= declared_count 2))
; Code transition: assert "(assert (not (= x 0)))" —
; "x" is a real declared variable referenced once
(assert (= real_var_refs 1))
; Code transition: regex \bnot\b matches the SMT "not" operator as if it were variable "not"
(assert (= keyword_op_refs 1))
; Code transition: referencedVars += 1 for each name in declaredNames found in the line
(assert (= referenced_vars_counted (+ real_var_refs keyword_op_refs)))
; Code transition: if referencedVars >= 2 → transitionCount++
(assert (=> (>= referenced_vars_counted 2) (= transition_count 1)))
(assert (=> (< referenced_vars_counted 2) (= transition_count 0)))
; Code transition: return transitionCount === 0 → false when transitionCount = 1
(assert (=> (= transition_count 1) (= result 0)))
; Violation: only 1 real variable is constrained; block is semantically vacuous
; but isVacuous returns false (non-vacuous), letting the vacuous block pass to Z3
(assert (= result 0))
(assert (= real_var_refs 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: variable reference appearing only in an inline comment on an assert line
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 59
; VIOLATION: variable reference appearing only in an inline comment on an assert line
; is counted as a genuine assertion reference, inflating referencedVars
(declare-const declared_count Int)
(declare-const expr_refs Int)
(declare-const comment_refs Int)
(declare-const referenced_vars_counted Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Code transition: 2 declared names {x, y}, passes size guard
(assert (= declared_count 2))
; Code transition: assert line "(assert (> x 0)) ; y is the budget"
; expression part references only x
(assert (= expr_refs 1))
; Code transition: comment "; y is the budget" contains declared name y;
; no comment-stripping occurs before regex search on the full line
(assert (= comment_refs 1))
; Code transition: referencedVars counts hits across entire line string
(assert (= referenced_vars_counted (+ expr_refs comment_refs)))
; Code transition: referencedVars >= 2 → transitionCount++
(assert (>= referenced_vars_counted 2))
(assert (= transition_count 1))
; Code transition: return transitionCount === 0 → false
(assert (= result 0))
; Violation: y appears only in the comment; y is unconstrained; block is vacuous
; but isVacuous returns false, passing the vacuous block to Z3
(assert (= comment_refs 1))
(assert (= expr_refs 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: multi-line SMT-LIB assertion — only the opening "(assert (" line is
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 59
; VIOLATION: multi-line SMT-LIB assertion — only the opening "(assert (" line is
; searched; variable references on continuation lines are invisible to isVacuous,
; causing a non-vacuous block to be falsely filtered out
(declare-const declared_count Int)
(declare-const first_line_refs Int)
(declare-const continuation_refs Int)
(declare-const referenced_vars_counted Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Code transition: declaredNames = {x, y}, size = 2 → passes size guard
(assert (= declared_count 2))
; Code transition: multi-line assert — only "(assert (and" enters asserts array
; neither x nor y appears on that opening line
(assert (= first_line_refs 0))
; Code transition: x and y appear on continuation lines that do NOT start with "(assert"
; → filtered out of asserts array → never searched
(assert (= continuation_refs 2))
; Code transition: referenced_vars_counted reflects only the first-line search
(assert (= referenced_vars_counted first_line_refs))
; Code transition: referencedVars < 2 → transitionCount stays 0
(assert (< referenced_vars_counted 2))
(assert (= transition_count 0))
; Code transition: return transitionCount === 0 → true (vacuous)
(assert (= result 1))
; Violation: continuation lines DO relate x and y; block is non-vacuous
; but isVacuous returns true, permanently suppressing a real verification result
(assert (> continuation_refs 0))
(check-sat)
